cards.title: Location context
cards.body: Neighborhood, nearby attractions, transport access, event venues, and reasons to stay.

cards.title: Guest fit
cards.body: Who the hotel is best for: business travelers, couples, families, events, wellness, groups, or local experiences.

cards.title: Offers and policies
cards.body: Offer names, inclusions, dates, price rules where approved, cancellation, payment, check-in, and booking paths.

items.question: What is hotel content for answer engines?
items.answer: It is content that clearly explains the hotel, location, rooms, offers, policies, experiences, and booking paths in a way that answer engines can understand and guests can use.

items.question: How is AEO different from normal hotel SEO?
items.answer: AEO focuses on making answers easy to extract, cite, compare, and act on. SEO still matters, but AEO puts more emphasis on entity clarity, structured pages, visible facts, and direct answers.

items.question: What hotel pages matter most?
items.answer: Property pages, room pages, offer pages, location guides, event pages, dining and spa pages, resource articles, and Partner storefront pages matter most.

items.question: Does structured data guarantee AI visibility?
items.answer: No. Structured data helps systems understand content, but it does not guarantee visibility. It should match visible content and sit on top of useful crawlable pages.

fields.heading: Ready to make hotel demand bookable?
fields.body: Start with the route that matches your team, then move into a focused demo or sign-up flow.
